flashcards.Back: A networking device that forwards data packets between computer networks. It operates at the network layer (Layer 3) and uses routing tables to determine the best path for traffic.
A hardware device that connects multiple devices on a single local area network. It uses hardware MAC addresses to forward data frames to the correct destination port.
A high-performance switch that combines traditional switching with routing capabilities. It can forward traffic based on IP addresses, enabling efficient inter-VLAN routing at hardware speeds.
The entry point for end-user devices into the local network. These switches provide high port density and basic security features like port security to protect the network edge.
A device that aggregates traffic from multiple access switches and connects them to the core layer. It typically implements routing, security policies, and traffic filtering.
A high-capacity backbone switch responsible for extremely fast transport of large volumes of data. Its primary goal is to minimize latency between different distribution layer modules.
A security appliance that provides advanced traffic filtering beyond traditional port and protocol inspection. It includes deep packet inspection and integrated intrusion prevention systems.
A network security technology that continuously monitors traffic for malicious activity. It can actively block or drop suspicious packets in real-time to prevent security breaches.
A hardware device that provides wireless connectivity to mobile clients within a local network. It acts as a bridge between the 802.11 wireless medium and the wired Ethernet infrastructure.
A centralized device used to manage and configure multiple lightweight access points. It simplifies wireless administration by handling radio frequency management and security policies.
A powerful network management and automation platform used for enterprise networks. It provides a centralized dashboard for design, provisioning, and troubleshooting via software-defined principles.
Any hardware device that marks the end of a communication path on a network. Common examples include laptops, smartphones, desktop computers, and printers.
A specialized computer or software program that provides specific services or data to other devices on a network. It typically hosts applications, files, or web content.
A technology that transmits both electrical power and data over standard twisted-pair Ethernet cabling. It allows devices like IP cameras to operate without separate power supplies.
A device, typically a PoE-capable switch, that provides electrical power to other components over Ethernet cabling. It detects compatible devices before delivering voltage.
A network component that receives its operating electrical power via an Ethernet cable. Common examples include VoIP phones, wireless access points, and security cameras.
A switch in a software-defined access network that connects directly to user devices. It handles the mapping of user traffic into the virtual overlay network.
A device in an SDN fabric that connects the internal automated network to external networks like the internet or a traditional data center. It manages traffic entering and leaving the fabric.
The 'brains' of an SDN fabric that maintains a mapping database of all endpoints and their locations. It allows the network to track mobile users and devices dynamically.
A device in a spine-leaf architecture that connects to both servers and every spine switch. It serves as the entry point for all traffic into the data center fabric.
A high-capacity backbone device in a fabric topology that interconnects all leaf switches. It does not connect directly to endpoints, focusing instead on high-speed internal switching.
A logical point-to-point connection used in SDN fabrics to transport user data across the physical underlay. It encapsulates original Ethernet frames within UDP packets for routing.
A specialized server that translates human-readable domain names into numerical IP addresses. This allow users to access network resources without remembering complex numbers.
A network service that automatically assigns IP addresses and other configuration parameters to client devices. It eliminates the need for manual static IP configuration on every host.
A computer system that stores and delivers web content to clients over the internet using HTTP or HTTPS protocols. It typically hosts websites and web-based applications.
A network device responsible for sending, receiving, and storing electronic mail. It uses standardized protocols like SMTP, POP3, and IMAP to handle user messages.
A physical object embedded with sensors and software that connects to a network to exchange data. Common examples include smart thermostats, industrial sensors, and connected appliances.
An endpoint device that transmits voice communications over an IP network instead of traditional copper phone lines. These devices frequently rely on PoE for power.
A legacy network management suite that provides life-cycle management for wired and wireless networks. It has largely been succeeded by the more modern Cisco DNA Center.
A passive hardware assembly used to organize and terminate multiple network cable connections. It serves as a central point for managing physical cabling in a server room.
